message_preview: The parser is written for the wrong JSONL schema. / The parser (cursor. py) looks for fields like: / • obj-get("timestamp", '") — top-level timestamp key / • obj.get ("session_id", '") - top-level ses
message_text: The parser is written for the wrong JSONL schema. / The parser (cursor. py) looks for fields like: / • obj-get("timestamp", '") — top-level timestamp key / • obj.get ("session_id", '") - top-level session_id key / • obj-get ("working_dir", ''") - top-level working_dir key / • obj-get("tool", ...) / obj.get ("name" ,..) - top-level tool name / • obj-get ("usage") - top-level usage/token block / But the actual Cursor agent transcript JSONL has a completely different structure: / {"role": "user", "message": {"content": K"type": "text", "text":"..."}]}} / {"role": "assistant", "message": {"content": ["type":"tool_use", "name": "Shell", "input": {"working_director {"type": "turn_ended", "status": "success"} / The mismatches: / Column / Parser looks for / Actual location in JSONL / timestamp / obj. timestamp / doesn't exist / session_id / obj. session_id / doesn't exist (it's the filename stem) / working_dir / obj. working_dir / nested at message.contentI]. input.working_directory / too l_name / obj. tool or obj. name / nested at message. content [l. name / message_text / obj. content or obj. text / nested at message. contentIl.text / input_tokens / obj. usage. input_tokens / doesn't exist / model / obj. model / doesn't exist
notes: bug report naming wrong fields; implies fix parser
